replies.msg: 咁英國有咩行動應對？

quote.msg:
Following today’s protests in Hong Kong, Foreign Secretary Dominic Raab said:

Whilst there is no excuse for violence, the use of live ammunition is disproportionate, and only risks inflaming the situation.

This incident underlines the need for a constructive dialogue to address the legitimate concerns of the people of Hong Kong. We need to see restraint and a de-escalation from both protestors and the Hong Kong authorities.

replies.msg:
1. "there is no excuse for violence" - Violence is not the problem. Aggression and coercion is. Violence in self-defence is not morally wrong, but physical assault with or without implements is.

2. The violence is instigated by the state. Without the state, the people are not violent. With the state, there is almost always some conflict involving violence.

3. Yes, shooting does not help the situation. The government is inflaming the situation.

4. Xi has demonstrated exactly zero intention of talking with the people of Hong Kong. The HK government has not had a genuine dialogue with the people of Hong Kong.

5. Because there is effectively no dialogue, there is no constructive dialogue. Maybe you can help arbitrate? Any other constructive ideas?

6. Restraint is not a solution to the problem, it only delays what is inevitable. The problem is the government refuses to negotiate anything.

7. Yes, the concerns of the Hong Kong people are legitimate, legal and moral. Many actions of the government are likely illegal. Many actions of the protesters are likely illegal too. However, there has been no effective recourse for the illegal actions of the government.

8. How is de-escalation on the part of the protesters a logical next step?

9. The violence of all governments are always disproportionate to the people.

10. Government believes that the gun is the final word.
However, it only works if people are afraid to die.
Many are not afraid anymore.

Dear Stephen,

We are a group of Hong Kong audience who appreciate your show very much. Your monologue is always entertaining yet it delivers important messages to the world on righteousness and freedom.

“Who kills its own kind on his birthday?”
CHINA.

We are writing to ask for your help as Hong Kong is now experiencing a serious humanitarian crisis. On 1st October, a HK police officer fired live rounds at a young protester’s chest. This protester is a high school student who is currently in ICU with a critical medical condition. Here’s the video footage:
https://youtu.be/KnAcFz20qAw

As you can see clearly from the video, the moment the police officer fired a bullet at the protester’s chest, the officer’s life was at no risk. The fact that he was marching towards the crowd with his gun pointing at them, instead of stepping backwards proves that he was not self-defending, but aggressive. The shooting was completely unreasonable, violent and brutal. This is undoubtedly a violation of human rights.

Hong Kong people need your help to spread the police brutality to the world because there’s nothing we can do to sanction these inhuman acts in Hong Kong. Please, you are our last hope. If you believe in freedom, if you believe in justice, if you believe the very basic human rights, please feature this shocking video in your next Late Show with Stephen Colbert.

Thank you for your kind attention.

Regards,
Hongkongers

[US immigration alert]

說明多元化移民簽證，又稱抽籤移民簽證或綠卡抽獎，是美國政府每年基於《移民與國籍法》第203條的規定為特定國家所舉辦的活動，吸引合資格申請人移民美國，在當地生活和工作，每年名額為50,000人。

The US Department of State annually administers the statutorily-mandated Diversity Immigrant Visa Program. For Fiscal Year 2021, 55,000 Diversity Visas (DVs) will be available.

There is NO COST to register for the DV program.

For DV-2021, mainland Chinese are not eligible to apply, because more than 50,000 PRC citizens immigrated to the United States in the previous five years. Persons born in HONG KONG SAR are eligible.

Applicants must submit entries for the DV-2021 program electronically at dvlottery.state.gov between noon, Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) (GMT-4), Wednesday, October 2, 2019, and noon, Eastern Standard Time (EST) (GMT-5), Tuesday, November 5, 2019.
